title: Why characters are named after ships
display_title: Why characters are named after ships
wikitext:
[]: Why characters are named after ships?             Quote Post by Shinji » Sat Nov 27, 2010 1:51 pm Aoba, Akagi, Ayanami, Fuyutsuki, Hyuga, Ibuki, Katsuragi, Kirishima, Makinami, Maya, Musashi, Soryu, and Shikinami are all names taken from Japanese war vessels. Why is this? I was hoping that Kyoko's name was a reference to Led Zeppelin, why all these naval references?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Nov 27, 2010 2:08 pm The better question is any of the characters ever realized this in a creepy Twilight Zone moment.

[]: Re: Why characters are named after ships?             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Nov 27, 2010 2:12 pm Shinji wrote: Aoba, Akagi, Ayanami, Fuyutsuki, Hyuga, Ibuki, Katsuragi, Kirishima, Makinami, Maya, Musashi, Soryu, and Shikinami are all names taken from Japanese war vessels. Why is this? I was hoping that Kyoko's name was a reference to Led Zeppelin, why all these naval references?

[]: Re: Why characters are named after ships?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Nov 27, 2010 2:53 pm esselfortium wrote: Not only that, but "Ikari" means "anchor," to further carry the maritime (er. not Mari-time ) theme.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sat Nov 27, 2010 5:05 pm It's an easy way to come up with surnames.

[]: Re: Why characters are named after ships?             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Nov 27, 2010 6:36 pm Shinji wrote: Aoba, Akagi, Ayanami, Fuyutsuki, Hyuga, Ibuki, Katsuragi, Kirishima, Makinami, Maya, Musashi, Soryu, and Shikinami are all names taken from Japanese war vessels. Why is this? I was hoping that Kyoko's name was a reference to Led Zeppelin, why all these naval references?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Nov 27, 2010 6:36 pm Actually, all the character surnames are taken from vessel's of their respective nationality, not just Japanese. Also: Anno has a passion for naval vessels.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sat Nov 27, 2010 8:54 pm Sachi wrote: Actually, all the character surnames are taken from vessel's of their respective nationality, not just Japanese. Also: Anno has a passion for naval vessels.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sat Nov 27, 2010 10:56 pm The writer wanted a theme for his characters' names? Imagine that! It's never happened before.

[]: Re: Why characters are named after ships?             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sat Nov 27, 2010 11:25 pm Shinji wrote: Aoba, Akagi, Ayanami, Fuyutsuki, Hyuga, Ibuki, Katsuragi, Kirishima, Makinami, Maya, Musashi, Soryu, and Shikinami are all names taken from Japanese war vessels. Why is this? I was hoping that Kyoko's name was a reference to Led Zeppelin, why all these naval references?

[]: Re: Why characters are named after ships?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 28, 2010 3:26 am supershinjiasukashipper wrote: Japan is a country surrounded by the sea...

[]: Re: Why characters are named after ships?             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun Nov 28, 2010 1:17 pm Mr. Tines wrote: From which commonality between the two archipelagos at either end of the Eurasian land mass we got the illustrious Mari Makinami.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Tue Nov 30, 2010 4:34 am Beccause Evangelion is highly referential to WWII, what with second impact being a representation of the Atomic bomb.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Nov 30, 2010 11:01 am I'm still waiting for Anno to recognise my country's WWII vessels, it'd be utterly fun. Or they simply didn't see enough actions and he doesn't even consider them?

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Tue Nov 30, 2010 5:05 pm Personally I am waiting for Anno to name a character after the Hood

[]:             Quote Post by Galaxy News Reidio » Wed Dec 01, 2010 8:00 am Fun little fact: In the Russo-Japanese war, there was a Maya class warship called Ritsuko .

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Dec 01, 2010 9:42 am Galaxy News Reidio wrote: Fun little fact: In the Russo-Japanese war, there was a Maya class warship called Ritsuko .

[]:             Quote Post by beaverteeth92 » Wed Dec 01, 2010 11:08 pm Anno picked warship names the same reason that Akira Toriyama picked vegetable names for Saiyans, instrument names for Piccolo's henchmen, and cold puns for Frieza and his family. Just because he could.

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Sat Dec 04, 2010 10:13 pm Kira Yamato It's lolzy

[]:             Quote Post by Assasinate » Sun Dec 05, 2010 1:41 am well apparently even WW2 is already finished, the Americans still fear the Japanese' utmost zeal in battlefield. i do inspire their BANZAI charge

Why is this? I was hoping that Kyoko's name was a reference to Led Zeppelin, why all these naval references?
